title: jarava subplumosa
intro: Jarava subplumosa, also known as Queñua or Mountain Queñua, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, characterized by its tall, evergreen stems and needle-like leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which are used to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Jarava subplumosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Jarava subplumosa are typically dried and infused in hot water to make a tea or decoction, which is used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to digestive issues and skin conditions.
